steps.title: Read the 2012/2013 data and the previous year
steps.content: In 2012/2013, Argentina is at approximately 70% (down from ~80% in 2011/2012), Brazil is at ~30%, and the United States is at ~8%. This establishes both Argentina's trend and the three-country ranking for that year.

steps.title: Check which choice matches this pattern
steps.content:
Choice C directly matches: Argentina decreased and remained the highest among the three.

The other choices each fail—A and B mischaracterize Brazil (flat value, not increasing, and never surpassing Argentina), while D incorrectly claims the US peaked in 2012/2013 when its ~8% is actually one of its lowest values on the graph.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
